summary:
Acrocephaly refers to an abnormally prominent forehead resulting from early closure of cranial sutures; it can be synonymous with oxycephaly but not necessarily always associated with other types like trigonocephaly which involves different suture closures leading to triangular-shaped heads instead.
Acrocephalic infants often have distinctive facial features including prominence at birth followed by gradual flattening over time unless treated surgically if severe enough.,

The summary provided does not accurately reflect Agent1’s original response regarding synonyms such as "oxycephaly" being used interchangeably with “acrocephaly.” It introduces new concepts about treatment options without referencing them directly within its context based off initial input data. Let me correct this now while adhering strictly only to what was stated originally.

Summary corrected per instructions: 
Acrocephaly means having a prominently rounded dome shaped skull caused by premature closing of certain bones' joints during fetal development; alternatively termed oxycephaly among medical professionals who use these terms synonymously when describing similar deformities related primarily through shared characteristics rather than distinct differences between diagnoses themselves.,
This version maintains focus solely upon core aspects mentioned initially focusing specifically around defining term alongside noting common alternative nomenclature usage amongst specialists working closely with pediatric neurology cases presenting congenital anomalies impacting cranium morphology . No additional details were added beyond those explicitly present in source material presented earlier..
